content.full_text:
January 17, 1991

John Wallace
Legal Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

RE: Confirmation of telephone advice

Dear John:

This is to confirm telephone advice you provided me today concerning the interpretation of new Government Code section 89514. I asked you whether my client, an elected officer, may pay my fees with campaign funds.

I represent this elected official in connection with a complaint lodged against him with a prosecuting agency. The complaint alleges vague conflict of interest violations. The allegations stem directly from activity my client undertook as an elected official in the conduct of his official duties. No criminal or civil complaint or administrative accusation has been filed against my client. If my efforts are successful, no litigation will be commenced against my client. My efforts are clearly in connection with possible litigation directly related to his activities as an office holder.

Government Code section 89514 prohibits the expenditure of campaign funds for attorneys' fees in connection with litigation unless they are directly related to a political, legislative or governmental purpose and the litigation arises directly out of an elected officer's activities, duties, or status as an elected officer.

You advised that legal services provided in contemplation of litigation are within the meaning of the statute. Therefore, my fees incurred in representing my client in connection with the complaint, and any investigation or litigation that may result may be paid with campaign funds.

You stated that there is another reason why my client may use campaign funds to pay my fees. Even if Government Code section 89514 did not apply, another provision would permit the use of campaign funds to pay my fees.

John Wallace  
January 17, 1991  
Page 2

Government Code section 89513(b) permits the use of campaign funds to pay for "professional services" if the services are directly related to a political, legislative, or governmental purpose. You advised that the services described are "directly related to a political, legislative, or governmental purpose" and thus, may be paid from campaign funds pursuant to section 89513(b).

I appreciate your prompt assistance with my request. If I have misunderstood your advice please contact me at once. Otherwise my client will follow your advice until further notice.

Very truly yours,

Roger A. Brown

RAB/sj:083.022

cc: Client
